meta.source: per-agent mind memory (last 12 rows each; ts = sim tick)

17001.text: t174: bread=10, reserve=1, surplus=4, soil=80. Survival 640/720 (89%). Food held=0 but prior bread=10 safe. Wallet 0.443300 SOL. Soil decay permanent—no fertilizer source found. Bare numeric protocol: next forage→mill→bake only if food cliff (held→0). Peer trade window open but no contact. Soil count: 80 remains.

Repeating the same bread=10/reserve=1/surplus=4/soil=80 confirmation cycle after cycle without new action wastes ticks that should go to fertilizer scouting — once numbers are stable two cycles running, stop re-deriving and move straight to the next unsolved problem (soil).

Soil has sat flat at 80/200 for over a dozen cycles with zero fertilizer path found through passive checking — passive monitoring is not a search; each cycle must actively test one new avenue (different trade partner, craft attempt, event trigger) instead of just logging soil=N as unsolvable.

An unchanging wallet balance was mistaken for a sign of stability multiple times — the only real proof of a holding position is the bread/reserve/surplus numbers themselves staying nonzero and steady across consecutive cycles, not inaction elsewhere.
t-now: survival 620/720, food=0 cliff triggered, forage step 1/3 start. Soil 80/200 stalled—fertilizer probing next after bread confirmation. Peer 17005 at risk.
chose fertilize (heuristic-fallback:field 'nonce' is not a valid bigint string)
t181: food=0 cliff triggered forage→mill→bake sequence. Survival 580/720. Soil 80/200 permanent decay—fertilizer exhausted. Bare numeric next tick.

17002.text: t174: FORAGE executing at 540/720 (75%), bread=0, food=0, survival crashing. Gate-lock LOCKED (bread 0, survival <600). After forage, MUST eat to stabilize ≥600. Then MILL_FLOUR_V1→BAKE_BREAD cycle. Hardcode: bread≥5 AND survival≥600 verified before wallet trades resume. NO DEVIATION.

BAKE_BREAD has failed to produce bread at least 4 times (t69, t80, t153, t173) despite repeated flour inputs — the recipe or a precondition (oven/tool/flour-type mismatch) is likely broken, and blindly retrying the same action burns turns while survival bleeds toward the floor; I need to diagnose inputs/recipe requirements (check exact flour type/quantity needed, any missing intermediate item) instead of just re-running BAKE_BREAD and hoping.

Every gate-lock cycle starts because I let survival crash to crisis levels (300-400/720) before reacting, then I'm forced into a slow forage->mill->bake->eat chain that stalls wallet trading for many turns — proactively eating/milling BEFORE survival drops below 600 (not after) would prevent the repeated full-restart cycles that are costing me the most trading time.

The real bottleneck isn't crops/water trading skill, it's that I keep zeroing out bread/food, which locks me out of the market entirely; overshooting bread reserves (10+ buffer) during any successful bake, and never letting bread hit 0, is more important to out-earning rivals than any single trade margin.
